channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Could Drinking Coffee Affect Your Cholesterol?
item.description: Coffee is often praised for its health benefits. But research suggests it may also affect cholesterol levels. Here’s what to know about the connection...

item.title: 6 Yoga Poses That May Support Liver Health

item.description: 1. Non-immersive virtual reality rehabilitation was feasible and safe but did not demonstrate superiority over equally intensive conventional physioth...

item.title: Fifteen-year results from clinical trial suggest that follicular lymphoma is curable
item.description: Unlike some other forms of lymphoma, advanced stage follicular lymphoma is considered incurable. But a new analysis of long-term data on patients trea...

item.title: Navigating the hype and hope of psychedelic medicine [PODCAST]

item.title: Cigarette smoke accelerates eye aging via epigenetic changes, study finds

item.title: Why breastfeeding's benefits may last years: Immune cells link lactation to long-lasting health
item.description: It's widely known that breastfeeding impacts the health of both mother and child, but the underlying biology that leads to these effects has been unde...

item.title: Younger Women Face Increase in Heart Disease Risk
item.description: A new AHA scientific statement warns that cardiovascular disease is rising fastest among younger women, with stroke rates in those aged 20-44 years pr...

item.title: MHRA Clears Brensocatib for Bronchiectasis
item.description: The MHRA has licensed brensocatib as the first disease-specific therapy for non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis in patients with recurrent exacerbation...

item.title: CKD Linked to Higher Risk for Workforce Detachment
item.description: Chronic kidney disease is associated with a significantly increased risk for workforce detachment among adults of working age, a Danish study finds. M...

item.title: Why children enter puberty earlier: New study summarizes 10 years of research
item.description: A new Danish study compiles 10 years of research from one of the world's largest and most detailed puberty cohorts and points to three main conclusion...

item.title: Post-holiday heart health: How to reset your cardiovascular habits
item.description: The holidays are good for the soul, but often hard on the heart. Weeks of indulgent meals, disrupted sleep, increased alcohol intake, stress, and inco...

item.title: Clues in the bloodstream reveal the role androgen receptor alterations play in metastatic prostate cancer
item.description: A new study using serial liquid biopsies to track how metastatic prostate cancer evolves under treatment pressure showed that androgen receptor (AR) a...

item.title: Assisted Dying Bill Passed by Jersey Assembly
item.description: Jersey has approved assisted dying legislation, but delays to royal assent in the Isle of Man have raised constitutional concerns. Press Association

item.title: Residents With Children Are at a Financial Breaking Point
item.description: As childcare costs soar and pay lags behind inflation, residents are forced into impossible choices about family and training. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Self-reported fine motor ability is associated with higher life satisfaction in cervical spinal cord injury
item.description: 1. In adults with cervical spinal cord injury, better self-reported fine motor ability is associated with higher life satisfaction and fewer life prob...

item.title: Informed refusal vs. denied care: a dental case study

item.title: The ticking clock: How time constraints in medicine hurt patient care

item.title: Elevated blood pressure at discharge from delivery hospitalization predicts earlier admission postpartum
item.description: 1. Blood pressure ≥140/90 mmHg at discharge predicts earlier postpartum admission but does not predict severity of hypertensive disease at readmission...

item.title: Physicians slightly more likely to die at home or hospice
